news.headline: NORBIT's segment PIR secures NOK 260 million orders from defence and security sector client. Deliveries to start immediately.
news.summary: In an announcement made in Trondheim on 14th March 2025, NORBIT reported that its PIR segment secured orders valued at around NOK 260 million from a confidential contract manufacturing partner operating in the defense and security field. The delivery process for these contracts will begin promptly, with a significant portion of the orders set for completion in the second quarter of 2025.

news.headline: US court approves Three Arrows to raise FTX claim to $1.53B from $120M, rejecting FTX's objections and citing delays in sharing info.
news.summary: A US bankruptcy court has approved an increase in the claim by liquidators of Three Arrows Capital (3AC) against FTX from $120 million to $1.53 billion. Chief Judge John Dorsey dismissed FTX's objections stating that the delay in amending the claim was due to FTX's failure to provide relevant information promptly. 3AC liquidators alleged breach of contract, unjust enrichment, and breach of fiduciary duty, claiming FTX held $1.53 billion in hedge fund assets liquidated to settle $1.33 billion in liabilities. Three Arrows Capital was one of the industry's largest crypto hedge funds with $3 billion in assets before its June 2022 collapse. FTX has also filed lawsuits to reclaim funds, including ones against SkyBridge Capital, Binance, and Waves founder. FTX filed for bankruptcy in November 2022.

news.headline: Basilea receives milestone payment due to robust Cresemba® (isavuconazole) sales in Asia Pacific and China.
Hong Kong Marriage Equality introduces "Proud Pair" to reshape traditions for diverse gender groups with jewelry.
news.summary: A historic 700-year-old tradition is undergoing transformation to embrace diversity in gender groups, with Hong Kong Marriage Equality teaming up with local jewellery brand Playback. The collaboration aims to promote equal marriage and spread love without boundaries. This initiative is set in motion in Hong Kong on March 14, 2025.

Trump cancels plan to double Canadian metal tariffs from 25% to 50% after Ontario removes charge on US electricity.
news.summary: The President had considered imposing a 50 percent tariff on Canadian metals; however, he decided against it following Ontario's removal of a charge on U.S. electricity.

news.headline: Trump's new tariffs on steel and aluminum, at 25%, cause global trade tensions as he aims to protect US economy.
news.summary: The recent move by President Trump saw the implementation of a 25 percent tariff on imports of metal, leading to escalating trade disputes worldwide. This action was taken as part of efforts to protect the U.S. economy from international competition.

news.headline: Galderma places EUR 500M Eurobond and CHF 435M bond, diversifies funding, reduces interest costs.
news.summary: Galderma Group AG, listed as SWX:GALD, operating in Zug, Switzerland, has reported notable advancements in its refinancing efforts. The company (SIX: GALD) has effectively issued a EUR 500 million bond with a fixed interest rate of 3.5% and a maturity period of 5 years. This move is part of their strategy to extend their maturity profile, broaden their funding channels, and lower interest charges, showcasing their commitment to financial stability and growth. The bond issuance is not intended for distribution in the United States or any jurisdiction where such actions are restricted by applicable laws or regulations.

news.headline: MEXC lists AO with a 140,000 USDT prize pool, expanding support for decentralized computing and AI innovation.
news.summary: Victoria, Seychelles - On March 14, 2025, MEXC, a prominent global cryptocurrency exchange, revealed its plans to list AO (AO) on the spot and futures markets starting at 06:00 (UTC). In celebration of this development, MEXC is initiating an Airdrop+ rewards event.

news.headline: Idorsia reports positive data on daridorexant's efficacy for chronic insomnia and nocturia in patients aged 55+.
news.summary: In Allschwil, Switzerland on March 14, 2025, Idorsia Ltd (SIX: IDIA) released a study titled “A randomized cross-over trial of daridorexant for the treatment of chronic insomnia and nocturia” in the Journal of Sleep Research. The report showcases the effectiveness of Idorsia’s daridorexant (QUVIVIQ™) at a 50 mg daily dose in individuals aged 55 years and above who have chronic insomnia and co-existing nocturia. The data highlights positive outcomes on symptoms for both conditions, enhancement in daytime functioning, and a favorable safety profile.

news.headline: Facephi excels in the DHS RIVTD Track 3 Evaluation in the USA, showcasing impressive performance.
news.summary: Facephi has achieved exceptional results in the RIVTD Track 3 Evaluation conducted by the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) of the United States. The evaluation, which focused on testing the performance of facial recognition technology, showcased impressive outcomes for Facephi's solutions. This recognition highlights the company's expertise and capabilities in the field, solidifying its reputation as a leader in the industry. The DHS evaluation serves as a testament to Facephi's dedication to innovation and excellence, positioning the company as a key player in the fast-evolving landscape of facial recognition technology.

Texas court declares default judgment against Bancor DAO after it ignored summons, investors claim USD tens of millions losses due to liquidity issues. DAO failed to defend itself, faces lawsuit for deceiving about ILP mechanism and unregistered security token. Case reopened after previous dismissal due to protocol developers not US-based. Plaintiffs argue DAO is unincorporated partnership; total value locked stands at $38M. Other similar cases set precedence for suing DAOs for unregistered securities.
news.summary: A federal judge in Texas has issued a default judgment against Bancor DAO for failing to respond to a class action lawsuit filed by investors in May 2023. The lawsuit claims that investors lost tens of millions of dollars due to Bancor's failure to warn about liquidity issues during a 2022 withdrawal spike. Plaintiffs allege that Bancor deceived investors about its impermanent loss protection mechanism and misrepresented its token as an unregistered security. The lawsuit also argues that Bancor DAO, described as an unincorporated general partnership, could be sued in that capacity. The case was previously dismissed but later reopened in December. Bancor is a decentralized exchange protocol with $38 million in total value locked, down 98% from its peak in May 2021. The ruling sets a precedent for similar cases involving decentralized autonomous organizations (DAOs) and unregistered securities.

news.headline: Earnings for Inspired Entertainment to be released on March 17. Expected earnings per share: 15 cents. Quarterly revenue: $79.19 million.
news.summary: Inspired Entertainment, Inc. (NASDAQ: INSE) will unveil fourth-quarter financial results before trading begins on Monday, March 17. Analysts anticipate the New York-based company will announce earnings of 15 cents per share, a decrease from 17 cents per share from the previous year. Inspired Entertainment is expected to report quarterly revenue reaching $79.19 million, down from $81.2 million in the corresponding period last year as per data from Benzinga Pro. On March 10, Inspired Entertainment disclosed an expanded partnership with Rush Street Interactive, Inc. (NYSE: RSI). The company's shares experienced a 6.4% decline, closing at $8.03 on Thursday. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.

Capco partners with OpenAI for AI innovation in financial services, enhancing solutions for top institutions.
news.summary: A management and technology consultancy, Capco, has revealed a partnership with OpenAI, a renowned developer of AI models like ChatGPT, GPT-4o, GPT-4.5, OpenAI o1, and o3 mini. The collaboration aims to enhance AI's influence in leading financial institutions globally. By merging OpenAI's advanced technologies and Capco's proficiency in financial services and GenAI, the collaboration will offer robust solutions to assist clients in achieving significant outcomes.

news.headline: Court grants temporary order stopping host from blocking Bitcoin miner's access to 21,000 rigs amid payment dispute. Source: Law360
news.summary: A Delaware court has granted a temporary restraining order to Bitcoin mining firm Consensus Colocation and its systems owner Stone Ridge Ventures against hosting company Mawson Hosting. The dispute arose over alleged unpaid fees and disagreements over the terms of their agreement. Mawson had reportedly been operating Consensus' 21,000 rigs, valued at $30 million, for gain since Feb. 28, generating daily profits of $100,000 to $200,000. The court order prohibits Mawson from using the rigs or blocking Consensus' access to them until a preliminary injunction hearing. The firms are set to terminate their partnership by March 2025, with Mawson claiming owed fees as a reason for redirecting the rigs' hashrate. Mawson believes they have the right to use the equipment under their agreement, while Consensus seeks injunctive relief to regain control. The temporary restraining order will remain in effect until the preliminary hearing.

news.headline: Lawsuit challenges AR rule banning foreign-born citizens from crypto mining. ACA takes action against state officials. Court documents reviewed. Unequal treatment alleged.编号AMA seeks to overturn AR law stopping foreign crypto miners. Suit filed against AG and Oil & Gas Commission director. Court bars enforcement.
news.summary: The Arkansas Cryptomining Association is suing state officials, including Attorney General Tim Griffin and Oil and Gas Commission Director Lawrence Bengal, for enforcing an unconstitutional state rule prohibiting foreign-born American citizens from engaging in crypto-mining. The complaint, filed in the US District Court, follows a federal court ruling that temporarily stopped Arkansas from preventing a naturalized Chinese citizen from operating a crypto mining business. The rules in question are “Rule K” and “Act 174,” which bar foreign-controlled businesses. The Association argues that these rules grant arbitrary powers to officials, enabling discriminatory enforcement. Director Connor Kempton highlighted violations of the 14th Amendment’s Equal Protection Clause and due process rights. The lawsuit claims that Act 174 infringes on the federal government’s authority to govern foreign investments. A court previously blocked officials from enforcing Act 174 against a specific mining business.

Rio Tinto donates A$1 million to aid Cyclone Alfred recovery in Queensland and New South Wales.
news.summary: In Melbourne, Rio Tinto has pledged a donation of A$1 million to the Australian Red Cross, an approved agency of the Queensland Government, in response to Tropical Cyclone Alfred. The funds are designated to offer emergency relief, aid in recovery efforts, and deliver long-term support services for communities in Queensland and Northern New South Wales affected by the cyclone. Rio Tinto's Chief Executive in Australia, Kellie Parker, expressed the significance of this support stating, “The effects of Tropical Cyclone Alfred are deeply personal to us, as one of...".

news.summary: Paul Grewal, Chief Legal Officer at Coinbase, is encouraging Congress to seize the opportunity of bipartisan support for comprehensive cryptocurrency legislation.

Coway's NOBLE 2.0 Air Purifier wins iF Design Award for 2025, marking their 18th honor in a row.
news.summary: SEOUL, South Korea - Coway has proudly received recognition from the iF International Forum for its NOBLE 2.0 Air Purifier Series due to its advanced design and cutting-edge air purification technologies. This achievement in 2025 signifies Coway's impressive 18th consecutive year of winning the prestigious iF Design Award.

news.headline: Singapore-based eco-packaging manufacturer, Green Lab, introduced eco-friendly packaging solutions in the US at cost parity.
news.summary: A prominent sustainability leader from Singapore, Green Lab, has introduced certified environmentally friendly packaging to the retail and foodservice sectors in the United States starting on March 14, 2025. As a subsidiary of Frasers &, Green Lab is a reputable eco-packaging producer in Southeast Asia. Their foray into the American market signifies a significant expansion, bringing their expertise and commitment to sustainability to a new audience. This move is poised to influence the adoption of greener practices in the industry.

news.summary: On March 14, 2025, in Bangkok, the Department of International Trade Promotion (DITP) of the Ministry of Commerce, along with the Board of Trade of Thailand, introduces STYLE Bangkok 2025, the highly awaited lifestyle and fashion trade fair in the region. This event will feature a showcase of the latest trends and products in the industry, with participation expected from numerous local and international businesses.

news.headline: Palace Museum in Beijing preserves China's 5,000-year-old civilization and culture, serving as a bridge between civilizations.
news.summary: In Beijing on March 14, 2025, the renowned Palace Museum, formerly known as the Forbidden City, stands as a crucial guardian of China's rich 5,000-year history and traditional culture. This institution serves as a vital repository, showcasing the heritage and essence of Chinese civilization. Its significance is underscored by its role in preserving and promoting the cultural legacy that spans millennia.

news.summary: Exports from China to emerging markets have experienced a significant increase, providing alternative pathways to reach the U.S. market. Authorities in Beijing are concerned that these routes could face potential closure due to the influence of President Trump.

news.headline: Kioxia unveils 122.88 TB NVMe SSD for AI apps, using 2 Tb QLC die, focusing on enterprise market.
news.summary: Kioxia, based in Tokyo, has recently unveiled a cutting-edge enterprise SSD designed for AI applications. This innovative storage solution boasts an impressive capacity of 122.88 terabytes and harnesses the power of Kioxia's 2 terabit QLC die technology.

news.headline: Bunker Hill Mining Corp amends vesting schedule for 3,891,096 RSUs, now vest on May 1, 2025 instead of March 13/March 31.
news.summary: In a recent announcement on March 13, 2025, Bunker Hill Mining Corp. disclosed changes to their restricted stock unit (RSU) vesting schedule. The Board of Directors approved an amendment for certain RSUs granted to directors and officers under the RSU Plan. A total of 3,891,096 RSUs from Prior Grants in November 2, 2022, July 4, 2023, and March 13, 2024, will now vest on May 1, 2025, instead of the previous dates of March 13, 2025, or March 31, 2025. The amendment applies only to the vesting date, with all other RSU terms remaining unchanged.

Discover how blockchain and federated learning transform AI governance, ensuring privacy and security for large-scale collaboration.
news.summary: Discover how the fusion of blockchain technology and federated learning is revolutionizing AI advancement with decentralized, privacy-centric management, facilitating extensive cooperation while safeguarding data integrity. This innovative approach has led to a significant increase in collaborative projects, with a noticeable rise in data breaches reduced by 30% last year. This shift has garnered attention due to its ability to ensure data privacy for users, with a projected 20% growth in adoption anticipated in the coming years.

news.headline: BetHarmony AI boosts iGaming efficiency with Advanced RAG, voice recognition, and multilingual features for operators to stay ahead.

BetHarmony has introduced a cutting-edge Advanced RAG (Retrieval Augmentation Model), along with voice automation and multilingual support, equipping operators with the necessary speed, intelligence, and flexibility to remain competitive. The developments were announced in Amsterdam on March 13th. These innovations aim to provide operators with the tools to stay ahead in the fast-paced iGaming industry.

news.headline: NovoPayment appoints Rodrigo Rodas as CEO and Rama Ituarte as CTO to drive fintech innovation in Latin America.

Guests can enjoy a buffet and other highlights throughout the evening. The venue address is 1180 Southlake Circle #100, Morrow, GA 30260.
